You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter China's most senior semiconductor executives issued a public call this week for a consolidated national effort to build a domestic alternative to Dutch lithography giant ASML, warning that the country's chip equipment industry remains too "small, fragmented, and weak" to overcome U.S. export restrictions on its own.Go deeper with TH Premium: Chipmaking (Image credit: tsmc)A deeper look at the chipmaking supply chainTSMC's $165 billion U.S. investments examinedChina reportedly reverse-engineers EUV toolChina bets on DUV, as EUV blockade reshapes chipmakingIn remarks co-authored by SMIC co-founder Wang Yangyuan alongside leaders of memory giant YMTC, chip equipment maker Naura, and EDA software developer Empyrean, three specific areas where U.S. export controls have choked China's semiconductor ambitions were identified: electronic design automation software, silicon wafers, and manufacturing equipment — particularly extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography, which enables the sub-7nm chip production that China currently cannot replicate.As reported by the South China Morning Post, the authors urged the industry to "abandon illusions and prepare for struggle," and argued that fragmented public funding was dispersing resources across too many competing efforts without producing results. You may like Chinese fabs are reportedly upgrading older ASML DUV lithography chipmaking machines U.S. lawmakers demand sales ban on chipmaking tools to China China's reverse-engineered Frankenstein EUV chipmaking tool hasn't produced a single chip China is currently drafting its 15th Five-Year Plan, which is set to be revealed to the National People’s Congress within the next week and covers 2026 to 2030. It’s widely expected that this iteration of the plan will prioritize lithography breakthroughs and EDA tool development as national targets. Big Fund III, a state-backed vehicle with roughly $47.5 billion earmarked for semiconductors, has already redirected fresh capital toward lithography and EDA as substitutes for ASML and Synopsys tools, respectively.The sheer candor about fragmentation from this collection of executives is difficult to ignore, however, given just how often we see big, bold tech claims coming from Chinese media, but it aligns with an increasingly obvious challenge. China's most advanced domestically produced DUV lithography system, from Yuliangsheng, is technically comparable to ASML's Twinscan NXT:1950i — a machine ASML originally designed for 32nm-class processes back in 2008.Even if SMIC manages to integrate that tool into a 28nm process by 2027, reaching sub-10nm would require redesigned scanners and several additional years of development. A prototype EUV machine has reportedly been completed in a Shenzhen lab, but EUV's commercial viability requires solving yield challenges that took ASML nearly two decades to overcome after its own prototype.That’s all before we’ve even considered ASML’s accumulated know-how as, in Yangyuan’s words, “merely the integrator.” The company’s EUV dominance rests on a supply chain of more than 5,000 subcontractors, along with decades of high-volume manufacturing data. No amount of reverse engineering can quickly replicate that. While it’s true that Chinese firms have made real gains in adjacent equipment categories — Naura, for example, is one of the world’s top ten semi equipment vendors by revenue — lithography remains well out of reach.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 中国顶级芯片高管称ASML替代品“小而散，还较弱”——产业大咖呼吁共同投入先进芯片制造工具
history.descriptionZh:
近日，包括中芯国际联合创始人王阳元、存储巨头长江存储、芯片设备制造商北方华创以及EDA软件开发商华大九天等中国半导体行业核心企业的高管们联合发声，公开呼吁举国之力构建荷兰光刻巨头ASML的国产替代方案。他们警告称，中国当前的芯片设备产业仍处于“小、散、弱”的状态，单靠自身力量难以克服美国出口管制带来的严峻挑战。

这份罕见的坦诚声明直指美国出口管制扼住中国半导体产业咽喉的三大关键领域：电子设计自动化（EDA）软件、硅片以及芯片制造设备，其中尤以极紫外（EUV）光刻机为甚。EUV光刻是实现7纳米及以下先进芯片制造的必需技术，中国目前尚无法自主生产。

**核心技术差距与挑战**

尽管在部分设备领域已取得进展——例如北方华创已成为全球营收前十的半导体设备供应商之一——但在作为芯片制造皇冠上明珠的光刻领域，特别是EUV，中国仍远远落后。报道指出，中国目前最先进的国产深紫外（DUV）光刻系统来自于上海微电子装备（SMEE），其技术水平大致相当于ASML于2008年推出的、针对32纳米级工艺设计的Twinscan NXT：1950i。即使中芯国际有望在2027年前将该设备集成到28纳米生产线中，要进军10纳米以下工艺，仍需对光刻机进行重新设计并投入数年研发。

更艰巨的挑战在于EUV。虽然有报道称深圳实验室已完成EUV原型机，但其商业化面临巨大的良率挑战，而ASML花费了近二十年才克服了这些难题。正如王阳元所言，ASML自身“仅仅是集成商”。其EUV技术的霸主地位建立在由超过5000家供应商构成的庞大供应链体系以及数十年高产量制造所积累的数据与经验之上。这种深厚的“软实力”与生态系统，绝非通过逆向工程就能在短期内复制。

**呼吁整合与未来规划**

高管们在声明中敦促业界“放弃幻想，准备斗争”，并批评当前分散的公共资金投入导致资源被稀释在太多相互竞争的项目中，难以形成有效突破。这一呼吁与中国即将推出的“十五五”规划（2026-2030年）方向一致。该规划预计将把光刻突破和EDA工具开发列为国家优先目标。与此同时，规模约475亿美元的国家集成电路产业投资基金三期（“大基金三期”）也已明确将资金导向光刻和EDA领域，旨在分别替代ASML和新思科技（Synopsys）的产品。

**总结**

此次行业高层的集体发声，罕见地承认了中国在突破高端芯片制造设备，特别是EUV光刻技术方面所面临的真实且巨大的鸿沟。它标志着中国半导体产业自主化进程进入一个更务实、强调集中资源攻坚关键瓶颈的新阶段。然而，前路依然漫长，不仅需要持续的巨额投资和技术攻关，更需要构建一个紧密协作、高度专业化的全球级供应链生态，这将是比制造出单台设备更为持久的挑战。

history.title: ‘CPUs are cool again,' Intel and AMD reporting spikes in CPU demand due to agentic AI, shortages — Lisa Su says business exceeded expectations while Intel is looking at long-term agreements with potential customers
history.description: Both AMD and Intel reports increased demand for CPUs, driven by AI agents' need for general computing power.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 10 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Both AMD and Intel said during the 2026 Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference that demand for CPUs is seeing an uptick due to artificial intelligence. Intel CFO David Zinsner said during his question and answer (via Investing.com) that “the CPU has become cool again this year,” especially as AI agents need CPUs to orchestrate the computationally-heavy tasks that the GPUs and NPUs will execute. It has even started seeing customers who are looking at long-term agreements, ensuring that they’ll have a continuous supply of these chips needed to expand their operations. On the other hand, AMD CEO Lisa Su said during the same conference, “You know, we’re seeing a significant CPU demand, frankly, as a result of the inference demand picking up.” She also added later that “the CPU portion of the business has actually far exceeded my expectations in terms of demand.”Go deeper with TH Premium: CPU (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU scaling with DLSSRyzen to the top: How AMD innovated in the gaming CPU marketHow ARM is working its way into PCsAMD CES 2026 gaming trends press Q&A roundtable transcriptThe AI boom has been driving a shortage in various components since ChatGPT showed some potential in late 2022. It first began with GPUs, especially as data centers and hyperscalers have been buying these components en masse to build massive servers with hundreds of thousands of GPUs. As graphics card supply started to normalize around the middle of 2025, experts and analysts have started warning about memory and storage chip shortages due to massive demand for high-bandwidth memory and enterprise-grade storage in AI data centers.We felt the full swing of this crisis in the fourth quarter of last year, with pricing for RAM modules and SSDs continuing to rise through February 2026. What makes this worse than the GPU shortage is that it has a much wider impact. While the GPUs that were in short supply were mostly limited to desktop PCs and gaming laptops with a discrete graphics card, virtually every modern digital device — from consumer devices like smart TVs and smartphones to automobiles and industrial-grade equipment — needs memory and storage. And consumer-grade memory and storage is fighting for the same wafer space that enterprise-grade memory and storage could occupy, usually with a far higher price tag. You may like Intel, AMD server CPUs reportedly suffering from supply shortages in China AMD Zen 6 and Intel Nova Lake CPUs reportedly arriving late, delayed to CES 2027 'We can't completely vacate the client market' says Intel amid wafer supply shortages As AI advancements move forward from large language models and chatbots to agents that can observe, reason, plan, act, and learn independently, data centers require more multi-processor computing power — that means combining CPUs, GPUs, NPUs, and more — to support the entire agentic AI workflow. China is starting to see this spike in demand, with both Team Blue and Team Red reporting supply shortages for server CPUs in the region. We’re also seeing a spike in demand for high-end Mac Studios and Mac minis, especially as we see more people build their own local AI agents with the rise in popularity of the open-source Clawdbot/Moltbot/OpenClaw.AMD and Intel are presumably talking about data center demand for their CPUs; consumer systems aren't equipped to handle the massive memory demands of agentic AI. If there is a shortage, however, it could trickle down to the consumer market, assuming the demand keeps pace.Over the past several generations, AMD and Intel have converged their data center and consumer offerings, allowing them to maximize yields by leveraging the same microarchitecture across both client and enterprise. Some of that silicon won't be useful in the data center, so the consumer market won't evaporate. But it could put downward pressure on supply if the focus shifts toward the data center, as we've seen with RAM and SSDs.Unlike Nvidia, which has seen exponential increases in revenue in its data center business, both AMD and Intel still see about half of their total revenue each quarter from the consumer market. It's still an important market, so although demand from data centers may increase, it shouldn't come at the cost of the consumer market, at least entirely.Hopefully, both Intel and AMD can keep up with the future demand, so as not to exacerbate the worsening situation of the computer industry. Otherwise, some are already predicting the end of the entry-level PC by 2028 if things continue as they are.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: CPU因AI代理需求激增又火了；苏姿丰称业绩超预期，英特尔寻求与潜在客户签长期协议
history.descriptionZh:
在2026年摩根士丹利技术、媒体与电信会议上，AMD和英特尔均指出，人工智能的蓬勃发展正在显著提振中央处理器（CPU）的市场需求。英特尔首席财务官大卫·津斯纳表示，“CPU在今年又重新变得炙手可热”，原因在于AI智能体需要CPU来协调和调度GPU及NPU所需执行的计算密集型任务。他甚至提到，部分客户已开始寻求签订长期供应协议，以确保业务扩张所需的芯片能得到持续供应。AMD首席执行官苏姿丰在同一会议上强调，“我们看到CPU需求显著增长，这实际上是推理需求攀升的结果”，并补充说“CPU业务部分的需求远超我之前的预期”。

这一需求的复苏根植于人工智能技术范式的演进。自2022年底ChatGPT展现潜力以来，AI热潮首先引发了GPU的严重短缺，数据中心和超大规模厂商大量采购以构建拥有数十万GPU的庞大服务器集群。随着2025年年中显卡供应开始趋于正常，专家和分析师转而预警，由于AI数据中心对高带宽内存和企业级存储的巨量需求，内存和存储芯片可能面临短缺。这一危机在2025年第四季度全面显现，RAM模组和SSD的价格持续上涨至2026年2月。与主要影响台式机和游戏笔记本的GPU短缺相比，内存和存储短缺的影响范围更广，波及从智能电视、智能手机到汽车和工业设备的几乎所有现代数字设备，因为消费级与利润更高的企业级产品需要在同一晶圆产能上竞争。

当前，AI技术正从大型语言模型和聊天机器人，向能够观察、推理、规划、行动和自主学习的智能体（Agent）发展。这导致数据中心需要更强大的多处理器协同计算能力，即结合CPU、GPU、NPU等异构单元来支持完整的智能体AI工作流。CPU在此过程中的核心角色是作为“协调者”，管理和分配任务给专用的加速芯片。这种转变直接推高了对服务器CPU的需求。中国市场已开始出现需求激增，英特尔和AMD均报告该地区服务器CPU供应紧张。同时，随着开源AI项目（如Clawdbot/Moltbot/OpenClaw）的流行，越来越多的人构建本地AI智能体，这也带动了高端Mac Studio和Mac mini的需求。

尽管AMD和英特尔提及的需求增长主要指向数据中心，但其供应链动态可能间接影响消费级市场。多年来，两家公司通过在其数据中心和消费级产品中采用相同的微架构以实现产量最大化。如果产能向利润更高的数据中心产品倾斜，就像之前在内存和存储领域发生的情况一样，可能会对消费级CPU的供应造成压力，导致短缺或价格上涨。与数据中心业务收入呈指数级增长的英伟达不同，AMD和英特尔每季度仍有约一半的营收来自消费市场，这是一个不容忽视的重要板块。因此，满足数据中心激增的需求不应以完全牺牲消费市场为代价。

分析师担忧，如果供需失衡持续加剧，可能会进一步恶化整个计算机行业的处境，甚至有人预测，到2028年入门级PC市场可能面临消亡。业界希望英特尔和AMD能够妥善应对未来的需求挑战，在抓住AI浪潮机遇的同时，维持消费市场的健康生态。

history.title: Go beyond the review with Bench, the deepest consumer hardware benchmarking database on the internet — compare hundreds of products across a range of categories
history.description: Take advantage of Bench, the ultimate resource for PC hardware enthusiasts, offering a large database of benchmarks for you to browse through. We detail how the tool works, and what we're doing to keep results up-to-date here.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter In 2025, we introduced Tom's Hardware Premium, an even deeper dive into the world of tech and DIY computing, which you can access through our website for a small fee. While that includes exclusive news analysis, features and hardware roadmaps, the Premium offering also sports a large database of benchmark test results, named Bench.Bench is a performance analysis tool that gives you deeper access into how a given product performs in real-world and synthetic benchmarks. Using it, you can compare two different products in a given category, offering you the ability to view benchmark data between products with a simple click.Bench is a Tom's Hardware Premium exclusive feature, and you can get access to it — as well as everything THP has to offer — by subscribing now for as little as $3 a month. You may like Get Tom's Hardware Premium for less than $3 a month — access our hardware roadmaps, deeper analysis, exclusive features, and unlock the Bench database Behind the scenes of our massive CPU retest for Bench — testing at 1080p, choosing new apps, and gathering data for a decade of CPUs Here are the tools I use as Tom’s Hardware resident CPU reviewer — a cheap aluminum open bench, thermal paste wipes, platform-labeled external power buttons, and more Tom's Hardware Premium Subcription: $29 at tomshardware.comDon’t miss out on Tom’s Hardware Premium. Get a full year of access for just $29: daily news analysis, deep dives into specialist topics in the semiconductor industry, as well as access to Bench, the largest benchmarking database around.View DealWith that out of the way, here's a sneak preview at what you can expect when subscribed. Let's take a look at a CPU comparison table to kick things off. Let's pit the AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D against Intel's Core Ultra 9 285K.First, all I have to do is hit the compare tab and select the category, which spans CPUs, GPUs, Laptops, Monitors, and SSDs. Once we're in the CPU category, we can select the two products. Then you'll see a comparison between all of the data points we recorded for those two CPUs in alphabetical order.Image 1 of 3First, we select the category.(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Once the products are selected, you can scroll through benchmarks.(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)There is a wealth of data to browse through.(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)As well as the comparison tool, Bench offers tables of data per category. So, if you're looking to see which part performs the best in a certain workload, you can do that too.For the next example, we're going to pick out a specific GPU workload and see which consumer graphics card performs best in that scenario. Let's take the Sony hit God of War: Ragnarok. If you are building a mid-range system, then 1440p or QHD is a good spot to select for this benchmark. Since we're focused on the mid-range, let's take a look at the middle of the table. Here, we can see that the Radeon 9060 XT is faster than Nvidia's RTX 5060. These two GPUs hit around the same price point, so if I were a God of War fanatic looking to save, that would be my pick.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Toms Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)How we run BenchBench offers tons of data to browse through; these are just two examples of how you can use the tool to browse through hardware benchmarks. While the database is primarily built from the work completed while reviewing a given product, the data that you see in reviews is often truncated into geomean values, or put into a certain workload "bucket."Of course, while Bench has been live since last year, benchmarks don't stay up to date: Drivers change. New standards emerge. Therefore, we're undergoing a large-scale overhaul of our GPU and CPU tests, documenting exactly how we are benchmarking and what methodologies are being used.Recording benchmark values and taking the time to run the tests in the first place is inherently time-consuming, and Bench is the perfect reason to maintain a source of truth for all enthusiasts. What to read next Here are the tools I use as Tom’s Hardware resident CPU reviewer — a cheap aluminum open bench, thermal paste wipes, platform-labeled external power buttons, and more Hands-on with the Thermal Grizzly Der8enchtable: Updating our longstanding motherboard test bench The GPU benchmarks hierarchy 2026 As for our testing methodologies, here's a glimpse of what's next for our massive database.Revamped data for GPU testing (Image credit: Future)On the GPU side of things, Senior GPU Analyst Jeffrey Kampman has the reins. He's worked out exacting testing methodologies and strategies and outlined them in a separate Tom's Hardware Premium article. For those who are not yet subscribed, we've explained why we're not using frame generation or upscaling technologies like DLSS and FSR, and how we conduct real-world benchmarks. A canned benchmark is handy, but it doesn't represent how it really feels to play a game on the hardware. And more important, it's a more accurate measure of what to expect when playing those titles for yourself.As Jeff explains, he's spent years testing GPUs in both media and dedicated game testing labs, ensuring the integrity of the results on offer. The spread of titles being tested for 2026 is also representative of what players are actually playing, not merely popular benchmarking titles. For example, we've added the single-player epic Expedition 33, in addition to the multiplayer hit Arc Raiders. Adding newer titles to the list, across a range of engines and with Ray Tracing, allows us to build a cohesive outlook on how a given GPU performs in a wide range of scenarios. (Image credit: Nvidia)Updates will be going in phases, first from current-generation hardware like Nvidia's RTX 5000 series and AMD's 9000 series, all the way back to RTX 3000 and the Radeon 6000 lineup. Of course, Intel's offerings are in the mix there too — no matter how small the market share of the dedicated GPU, it'll be being tested for 2026.The three waves of updated tests will take around a month to fully complete, but will offer you a holistic look at the GPU market, and more importantly, offer you granular testing information that you simply can't get anywhere else.A herculean CPU retesting effort (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU Analyst Jake Roach is working alongside Editor-in-Chief and former CPU reviewer Paul Alcorn to deliver a gargantuan update to our CPU tests. Their goal is building a complete dataset of CPU releases that spans over a decade of hardware. For Premium subscribers, you can read Jake's entire methodology behind CPU testing here.Between two and six machines are running concurrently, with two Golden OS images, frozen and duplicated across setups, using the same testing hardware, down to the TIM, to ensure consistent results. Of course, as we are currently testing different platforms, the motherboards used between CPUs differ, but are consistent between rigs.While we are testing hundreds of CPUs, everything from the version of the software to the processes running in the background are identical. For the CPU effort, we'll be going from AMD Excavator and 7th-gen Intel to Zen 5 and Intel Arrow Lake. That's a lot of CPUs, and even more benchmarks. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Each modern CPU takes between 10 and 14 hours to test, with some older and slower models taking nearly an entire day, and with the number of chips on offer, it's going to take some time to get things up-to-date. As for the tests we run, things are split into several categories, such as applications, power/efficiency, and gaming workloads. While some tests are automated, each result is manually verified before being uploaded into our database and tools.Application testing goes across a wide range of workloads, from rendering and encoding to code compilation and data science. We're testing power efficiency across benchmarks like Y-Cruncher, Handbrake, and Prime95, just to name a few. Gaming testing is hands-on where possible. The selection of titles is broad and represents real-world players and what people are playing.As a result, no, you're not going to see something like Ashes of the Singularity, which no one plays, but is merely used as a benchmark. Instead, gaming benchmarks are selected across a range of popular titles like Monster Hunter: Wilds, DOOM: The Dark Ages, and Oblivion: Remastered, all of which use wholly different engines, and are being played by many today.Results will begin to trickle out across the database over time, and given the scale of the tests on offer, we will continue to evolve the benchmarking suite, while acknowledging that we might not revisit some of the older architectures on this list again.The benchmarks never stop (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)On the backdrop of a challenging year for consumer hardware, no thanks to the ongoing AI buildout and supply challenges. Getting the most out of what's available is imperative, and Bench gives you access to all of the data you need for whatever workload or game you're looking at within a handful of clicks.We've put a lot of effort into building and maintaining this feature, and we will continue to do so, documenting the process and keeping things nice and fresh for Tom's Hardware Premium subscribers.Maintaining Bench without Tom's Hardware Premium simply isn't possible. It offers us the bandwidth to test, retest, and refine our methodologies outside of a standard CPU cycle or hierarchy update. It also becomes an invaluable reference point for those of you who wish to seek out specific hardware workloads, going beyond our standard reviews and wading through the data yourself.We're never going to stop testing, and Bench is the perfect place to compile our data. As we look forward to the next generation of PC hardware, we'll be sure to keep things up-to-date, giving you access to the cold, hard data behind our testing. In addition to our testing updates, we will also keep working behind the scenes to add new functionality to Bench in the coming months.Tom's Hardware Premium is priced at $3 a month when taking out an annual plan, or $7 for a rolling monthly subscription. Bench is just a single piece of what's on offer. To take advantage of the ultimate resource for enthusiasts, be sure to check it out today.
history.author: The Editors of Tom's Hardware
history.titleZh: 不止于评测，利用互联网上最深度的消费级硬件数据库Bench——对比数百款各类产品。
history.descriptionZh:
Tom's Hardware作为领先的硬件评测媒体，于2025年推出了付费订阅服务"Tom's Hardware Premium"，其核心价值在于提供了名为"Bench"的深度性能分析工具与数据库。该服务旨在为科技爱好者和DIY用户提供超越常规评测的硬件性能洞察，年费为29美元或每月3美元。

**核心技术原理与创新点**
Bench本质上是一个结构化性能数据库，其创新性体现在两方面：
1. **动态对比引擎**：用户可自由选择CPU、GPU、笔记本等品类中的任意两款产品，系统自动生成数十项测试数据的直观对比。例如AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D与Intel Core Ultra 9 285K的全面比拼，所有参数按字母序排列，支持一键切换查看。
2. **场景化数据表**：针对特定应用场景（如《战神：诸神黄昏》游戏在1440p分辨率下的表现），系统会生成该品类产品的性能排序表。数据显示Radeon 9060 XT在同价位段领先于RTX 5060，为装机选择提供精准参考。

**测试方法论革新**
面对驱动程序更新与行业标准演进，编辑部正在开展大规模测试体系重构：

**GPU测试升级（由高级GPU分析师Jeffrey Kampman主导）**
- 摒弃传统基准测试工具，采用真实游戏场景测试，排除DLSS/FSR等画质增强技术干扰
- 测试游戏库兼顾引擎多样性与玩家实际偏好，新增《远征33》等单人史诗游戏与《Arc Raiders》等热门联机游戏
- 测试范围覆盖从最新RTX 5000/Radeon 9000系列到上代RTX 3000/Radeon 6000系列的三波更新，预计耗时一个月

**CPU十年数据重构（由CPU分析师Jake Roach与主编Paul Alcorn负责）**
- 建立跨越十年的完整CPU数据集，从AMD挖掘机架构/第七代酷睿覆盖至Zen 5与Arrow Lake
- 采用标准化测试环境：2-6台并行测试机、统一的黄金系统镜像、连导热硅脂都保持一致
- 每颗现代CPU需10-14小时测试周期，老旧型号需近24小时
- 测试类别涵盖应用程序（渲染/编码/代码编译）、能效（Y-Cruncher/Handbrake/Prime95）及游戏性能

**行业影响与应用价值**
在当前AI基建扩张导致消费级硬件供应紧张的背景下，Bench的价值凸显：
1. **评测范式转变**：彻底改变以《奇点灰烬》等纯基准软件为主的传统模式，完全聚焦《怪物猎人：荒野》《毁灭战士：黑暗时代》等真实游戏表现
2. **数据权威性**：通过严格控制的测试环境与手动验证机制，建立硬件性能的"单一事实来源"
3. **长期维护承诺**：订阅制商业模式为持续测试提供资金支持，确保数据库随硬件迭代保持更新

**服务定位与展望**
Bench仅是Tom's Hardware Premium服务的组成部分之一，该订阅还包含独家新闻分析、硬件路线图等深度内容。编辑部计划未来数月持续增加Bench新功能，在下一代PC硬件浪潮中保持数据库的时效性与完整性。对于追求极致数据透明度与深度分析的专业用户，该服务提供了超越常规评测的决策支持工具。

history.descriptionZh:
在当前PC硬件市场价格高企的背景下，捆绑销售成为购买高端组件如内存、显卡和固态硬盘时节省开支的主要方式。近日，Newegg推出了一款新的组合套餐，与之前的优惠类似，但此次采用了微星（MSI）一款价格更亲民的主板。

该套餐包含三件核心组件：微星MAG X870 Tomahawk WiFi主板、32GB的 Corsair Vengeance RGB DDR5-6400 内存（2×16GB），以及AMD Ryzen 7 9850X3D处理器，套装价格为999.99美元。相较于组件单独购买的总价1196.98美元，消费者可节省196.99美元。在目前PC硬件整体昂贵的市场环境下，以低于1000美元的价格获得这三样高性能组件，无疑是一个颇具吸引力的选择。

**核心技术规格与创新点：**
1.  **微星 MAG X870 Tomahawk WiFi 主板**：支持PCIe 5.0高速接口，提供四个M.2插槽，后置I/O面板配备了两个40Gbps的USB-C Lightning端口。主板支持DDR5内存，并设计了无需工具的显卡快速释放开关，同时集成了高速的WiFi 7无线连接功能。
2.  **海盗船 Vengeance RGB DDR5-6400 内存**：这套32GB（双通道）内存的速率为6400 MT/s，这对于AM5平台的AMD处理器而言是一个性能与稳定性俱佳的“甜点”频率。受人工智能热潮推动，该内存套件在过去四五个月中价格已上涨超过一倍，单独购买价高达437美元。但在此捆绑套餐中，其等效价格仅为241美元，虽然仍不便宜，但远低于市场零售价。
3.  **AMD Ryzen 7 9850X3D 处理器**：这款基于AM5平台的处理器拥有8核16线程，通过小幅提升功耗，其游戏性能已超越前代9800X3D，成为当前最快的游戏CPU。基准测试显示，9850X3D的性能领先9800X3D，但优势幅度有限。

**套餐价值与附加优惠：**
除了三大核心硬件，购买此套装还可获得两份“免费”赠品：价值55.99美元的华硕TUF Gaming M4 Air轻量化游戏鼠标，以及标价70美元的RPG游戏《Crimson Desert》。这进一步提升了套餐的整体价值。

**技术影响与应用场景：**
该套餐精准定位高端游戏PC装机市场。以999.99美元的价格，用户即获得了构建一台新高端游戏电脑所需的三大核心部件（CPU、主板、内存）。用户只需额外添加固态硬盘、显卡、机箱、电源和散热系统，即可完成整机组装。对于追求顶级游戏性能，同时又希望控制预算的玩家和DIY爱好者来说，这是一个高效且经济的入门方案。

**总结：**
Newegg此次推出的微星主板、海盗船内存与AMD旗舰游戏处理器的捆绑套餐，通过显著的直接折扣和附加赠品，在高端硬件市场中提供了难得的性价比。它不仅降低了组建顶级游戏平台的初始门槛，也反映了在当前市场环境下，捆绑销售对于消费者和零售商而言是一种双赢的策略。

history.title: Nvidia reportedly working on RTX 5050 with 9GB of VRAM on a 96-bit bus, featuring 28 Gbps GDDR7 modules — RTX 5060 with cut-down GB205 GPU also planned
history.description: Nvidia is reportedly working on a new RTX 5050 with 9GB of GDDR7 VRAM, up from 8GB GDDR6 on the existing model. Moving from 20 Gbps chips to 28 Gbps chips, the memory interface is also said to be reduced to 96-bit, from 128-bit on the original. Moreover, an RTX 5060 with a cut-down GB205 die is also said to be in the works, with otherwise same specs.

Subscribe to our newsletter The global component shortage is apparently affecting Nvidia — one of the catalysts of this very crisis — just as hard as other manufacturers, with the consumer segment taking the hit. Memory and chip constraints are leading the company to think of interesting new products — a new RTX 5050 with 9GB of VRAM seems to be in the works, according to leaker @Zed__Wang.New product: RTX5050 9GB GDDR7 96BitNV knows they can give you a 5050/5060 128-bit 12G with the new 3G GDDR7 dies. But no, you got a 5050 9G💀March 4, 2026According to the tweet, this new RTX 5050 will adopt 28 Gbps GDDR7 memory chips in favor of the existing 20 Gbps GDDR6 modules. The VRAM capacity will be upped from 8GB to 9GB, but the bus width will be cut down from 128-bit to just 96-bit. The current spec translates to 320 GB/s of bandwidth, and the updated spec will result in 336 GB/s bandwidth, which is a 5% increase.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PCThe leaker goes on to mention that Nvidia could build 12GB variants of the RTX 5050 and 5060 with 3GB GDDR7 chips, but perhaps the company isn't concerned with value maximization like that, especially not in these times. The last time a desktop GPU from Nvidia rocked a 96-bit interface was back in 2024 with the RTX 3050, so it's actually not too distant of a memory. The rest of the specs should remain identical; the same GB207 die with 2,560 CUDA cores, built on TSMC's 5nm process, rated at 130W. Clock speed differences (if any) will become public knowledge as we near the potential release of this SKU. The RTX 5050 is one of the few GPUs that basically saw no price hikes in the past few months, only going up about $10. The only other card with the same stability was the RTX 5060. You may like Nvidia RTX 5070 mobile GPU looks set to get more VRAM despite global memory crisis Modders are slapping 32GB of VRAM on the RTX 5080, which isn't good for gamers Micron joins the 3GB GDDR7 party, introduces 36 Gbps modules for GPUs RTX5060 GB205 incomingNV has jammed AIC with 5060Ti 8G, and later realized Oh shit, no GB206 for 5060. And here comes the solution: 5060 based on GB205. Poor AICs need to make a new 5060 PCB design to house the GB205. Basically making a 5070 PCB with an 8-pin connector.March 4, 2026Speaking of which, @Zed__Wang also talked about a new RTX 5060 in the works with a cut-down version of the GB205 GPU — that's the silicon that powers the RTX 5070. Apparently, Nvidia has told AIBs to focus on the 8GB RTX 5060 Ti, which has led to a shortage of GB206 dies for RTX 5060 SKUs. Since both the RTX 5060 and 5060 Ti use the GB206, the chipmaker is now forced to switch gears.Defective GB205 dies might be repurposed to fit into new PCB designs for the RTX 5060 with an 8-pin power connector. Otherwise, the RTX 5070 has a 12V-2x6 connector and the GB205 die inside features 6,144 CUDA cores — those would be reduced to 3,840 CUDA cores for an RTX 5060. Moreover, the bus width on the GB205 is 192-bit and that would also go down, to 128-bit, in order to match an RTX 5060. history.titleZh: 据报道英伟达正在开发RTX 5050显卡，配备9GB显存与96位总线，搭载28Gbps GDDR7显存模块——同时规划采用精简版GB205 GPU的RTX 5060。
history.descriptionZh:
全球零部件短缺的影响已波及英伟达——这一危机的主要推手之一——其程度不亚于其他制造商，消费级显卡市场首当其冲。内存与芯片供应限制正促使该公司构思有趣的新产品：据爆料者@Zed__Wang透露，一款配备9GB显存的新款RTX 5050似乎正在酝酿中。

根据推文信息，这款RTX 5050将采用28 Gbps的GDDR7显存芯片，取代现有的20 Gbps GDDR6模块。显存容量将从8GB提升至9GB，但显存位宽将从128位削减至仅96位。当前规格的带宽为320 GB/s，更新后的规格将带来336 GB/s的带宽，增幅约为5%。

爆料者进一步指出，英伟达本可利用新的3GB GDDR7芯片打造12GB显存版本的RTX 5050和5060，但该公司或许并不热衷于如此追求价值最大化，尤其是在当前时期。英伟达上一次在桌面GPU上采用96位接口还要追溯到2024年的RTX 3050，因此这并非遥远的记忆。其余规格预计保持不变：同样基于台积电5纳米工艺的GB207芯片，拥有2560个CUDA核心，热设计功耗为130W。时钟频率的差异（如果有）将在这款SKU临近发布时公之于众。RTX 5050是过去几个月内为数不多价格基本未上涨的显卡之一，仅上涨约10美元。唯一另一款价格如此稳定的显卡是RTX 5060。

与此同时，@Zed__Wang也谈及了正在开发中的新款RTX 5060，它将采用精简版的GB205 GPU——即驱动RTX 5070的芯片。显然，英伟达已告知AIB合作伙伴专注于8GB显存的RTX 5060 Ti，这导致了用于RTX 5060系列SKU的GB206芯片短缺。由于RTX 5060和5060 Ti都使用GB206，英伟达现在被迫转变方向。

有缺陷的GB205芯片可能会被重新利用，装入为RTX 5060设计的新PCB板中，并配备8针电源接口。否则，RTX 5070使用的是12V-2x6接口，且其内部的GB205芯片拥有6144个CUDA核心——为适配RTX 5060，这一数量将削减至3840个。此外，GB205的192位显存位宽也将相应降低至128位，以匹配RTX 5060的定位。

总体而言，这些潜在的产品调整揭示了英伟达在显存与核心芯片供应双重压力下的灵活应对策略。通过调整显存配置（如采用非标准的9GB容量与96位宽组合）以及在芯片层级进行产品线重塑（将更高级别的缺陷芯片降级使用），英伟达试图在维持产品迭代的同时，应对供应链的不确定性。这些举措对消费者意味着，未来可能会看到更多在显存容量、位宽和核心配置上打破传统规律的显卡型号，其性能提升可能有限，但旨在保障市场供应。对于追求极致性价比或特定显存需求的用户而言，理解这些产品背后的供应链逻辑将变得更加重要。

history.title: Seven tech giants signed Trump’s pledge to keep electricity costs from spiking around data centers
history.description:
Trump summoned tech leaders to the White House on Wednesday, March 4, 2026 to sign pledges committing their companies to foot the electricity bill for energy-hungry data centers.  | Photo: Getty Images	

Leaders from Google, Meta, Microsoft, Oracle, OpenAI, Amazon, and xAI met with President Donald Trump today to sign a "rate payer protection pledge." It's one way they're responding to growing bipartisan concerns about electricity rates rising as tech companies and the Trump administration rush to build out a new generation of AI data centers. 
"[Tech companies] need some PR help because people think that if a data center goes in, their electricity prices are going to go up," Trump said during the event. "Some centers were rejected by communities for that and now I think it's going to be the opposite."

history.titleZh: 七家科技巨头签署特朗普承诺，防止数据中心周边电力成本激增
history.descriptionZh:
2026年3月4日，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普召集了来自谷歌、Meta、微软、Oracle、OpenAI、亚马逊及埃隆·马斯克的xAI等七家顶尖科技公司的领导人赴白宫，共同签署了一份名为“费率支付人保护承诺”的协议。此举旨在回应美国两党日益增长的共同关切：随着科技公司与特朗普政府大力推动新一代人工智能数据中心的建设浪潮，社会担忧这将导致居民和企业电价大幅上涨。

此次事件的核心背景在于，AI数据中心是极为耗电的基础设施，其大规模扩张对地方电网构成了巨大压力，并可能推高周边社区的用电成本。这种担忧已经导致一些社区抵制数据中心的建设计划。为解决这一矛盾，特朗普政府与科技巨头协商出台了此份承诺，其核心创新点在于商业模式与责任划分：科技公司自愿承诺承担其数据中心带来的额外电网升级和供电成本，确保当地居民和企业的电价不会因此上涨。特朗普在签署仪式上表示，科技公司需要“公关帮助”，以扭转公众认为数据中心推高电价的观念，并预言此举将使数据中心从被社区拒绝转变为受欢迎。

从影响层面看，该协议具有多重意义。首先，在政治与社会层面，它试图缓解因AI基础设施建设引发的公共争议，为数据中心的快速部署扫清民意障碍。其次，在经济层面，它将潜在的公共成本外部性内部化，由直接受益的科技公司承担相关电力基础设施投资，保护了普通纳税人和企业的利益。最后，在技术发展层面，该协议为美国AI产业的爆炸式增长提供了稳定的政策环境和资源保障，确保了其所需庞大算力基础的持续扩容，同时避免了与民争电的社会矛盾。

这一承诺的应用场景直接指向遍布全美各地规划或建设中的大型、超大型AI数据中心。它代表了一种新的公私合作模式，即由私营资本主动解决其扩张带来的公共基础设施压力。虽然协议的具体财务细节和长期执行机制尚未完全公开，但它标志着在AI时代，基础设施建设的成本分摊模式正在发生重要演变，科技公司除了技术竞争，也必须在承担社会责任和维护公共关系上做出明确承诺。

history.title: Taiwan expects power demand to increase by more than 5GW by 2030, enough to power nearly 4 million homes — rise in electricity consumption driven by semiconductor manufacturing and AI data center deployments
history.description: New semiconductor fabs and data centers are fueling Taiwan's growing power demand, with the island expected to require more than 5GW of new electricity sources and power infrastructure to keep up.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Taiwan is facing a new challenge in its goal to retain its advantage in AI and advanced semiconductor manufacturing — crucial for maintaining its silicon shield — as it grapples with a tightening power supply. Electricity demand is rising sharply as Taiwanese tech companies build semiconductor manufacturing facilities alongside AI data centers. According to Digitimes, both the AI and semiconductor industries could add about 5 gigawatts of demand — enough to power upwards of 3.75 million homes — by 2030, roughly a gigawatt of additional load per year through the end of the decade.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThis expansion, fueled by increasing global demand for the most advanced AI chips, is putting even more pressure on the island’s grid. Taipower chairperson Wen-sheng Tseng notes that each wafer fab needs around 200MW to operate, suggesting that the multiple facilities planned for and being built around the island will demand 5.3 to 5.4 gigawatts of electricity. This does not count the other plants and factories needed to support the operation of these factories, so it’s likely that the power demand will even be higher than projected. Nevertheless, four new electricity-generating gas units are set to come online this year, with an additional 5.2GW expected to enter trials soon. The company is also working to beef up Taiwan’s grid and enhance resilience plans, ensuring that the island’s power supply can keep up with the demand, particularly from science parks and industrial zones.Despite that, Taipower is still warning that demand is also increasing outside of these areas. Some fabs and servers are setting up facilities beyond these designated sectors, sometimes even requesting up to 20% of the power consumption of a science park for just a single structure. Because of this, Tseng says that both fabs and tech companies should be careful when selecting sites for their future infrastructure, ensuring that they’ll only be built in places where there is ample power supply to avoid bottlenecks in the future. You may like U.S. electricity grid stretches thin as data centers rush to turn on onsite generators Anthropic promises to pay for electricity price increases due to it's AI data centers Nvidia-backed trial shows AI data centers can flexibly adjust power use in near real time, with global implications for energy consumption Even though Taiwan’s power utility company is warning about power consumption, it seems to be on track to cover the future growth of the AI and semiconductor industry, provided that the current projections hold. This is a far cry from what the U.S. is experiencing, with the White House resorting to forcing big tech companies to promise that they will pay for the grid and power source upgrades needed to supply the massive amounts of electricity that AI data centers need. history.titleZh: 台湾预计到2030年电力需求将增加逾500万千瓦，足以供应近400万户家庭用电——用电量增长主要来自半导体制造和人工智能数据中心部署
history.descriptionZh:
台湾正面临一场新的能源挑战，这对其保持人工智能（AI）与先进半导体制造优势、进而巩固其“硅盾”的战略目标构成了直接威胁。随着全球对尖端AI芯片需求的激增，台湾科技公司正在大力建设半导体制造设施和AI数据中心，导致电力需求急剧攀升。

根据《电子时报》的报道，预计到2030年，仅AI和半导体产业就将新增约5吉瓦（GW）的电力需求，相当于超过375万户家庭的用电量，这意味着直至本年代末，每年将增加约1吉瓦的负荷。台湾电力公司董事长曾文生指出，一座晶圆厂大约需要200兆瓦（MW）的电力来运作。目前全岛规划中和建设中的多个大型制造设施，预计将共同带来5.3至5.4吉瓦的电力需求，而这还不包括支持这些工厂运作所需的配套厂房与设施，实际总需求可能比预估的更高。

为应对这一压力，台湾电力公司正积极采取措施。今年将有四个新的天然气发电机组投入运营，预计还将有总计5.2吉瓦的新增发电容量很快进入试运行阶段。同时，公司致力于加强电网建设和提升系统韧性，以确保电力供应能够跟上科学园区及工业区的需求增长。

然而，挑战不仅限于此。台电警告称，电力需求的增长已蔓延至传统规划区域之外。一些芯片制造厂和服务器设施选择在非指定区域建厂，单个建筑的用电需求有时甚至高达一个科学园区总耗电量的20%。因此，曾文生建议，芯片制造商和科技公司在为未来基础设施选址时必须谨慎，确保只建立在电力供应充足的地方，以避免未来出现瓶颈。

从更广泛的影响来看，台湾电力供应的紧张态势直接关系到其作为全球半导体与AI制造重镇的核心竞争力。充足的能源是维持先进制程研发与大规模制造的根本前提。尽管台电的规划显示其有望满足产业未来的增长需求（前提是当前预测准确），但这一能源压力已然凸显。相比之下，美国面临的局面更为严峻，白宫甚至不得不采取强制措施，要求大型科技公司承诺为满足AI数据中心巨大耗电而必需的电网和电源升级支付费用。

总体而言，台湾正在其至关重要的高科技产业发展与日益紧张的能源供应之间寻找平衡。能否成功保障稳定、充足的电力，将成为其能否维持“硅盾”优势、继续引领全球AI与半导体产业发展的关键决定因素。

history.title: Trump summons tech giants to White House to pledge power payment commitments — ‘ratepayer protection plan’ will make data center operators negotiate discrete payment structure for electricity use
history.description: Some of the U.S.'s biggest AI tech companies are expected to go to the White House to sign the "ratepayer protection pledge" that promises they will pay for all of their electricity usage and not pass on the burden of increased rates to the average American.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 19 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter The biggest AI tech companies, including Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, and Google, are expected to join President Donald Trump at the White House today to sign the new “ratepayer protection pledge” in a bid to keep electricity prices under control. Trump said during the State of the Union address, “We’re telling the major tech companies that they have the obligation to provide for their own power need.” According to Bloomberg, Meta, Amazon, and OpenAI have confirmed attendance at the White House event on Wednesday, while Google declined to comment.The massive power demands of the AI data centers have upended the electricity market, causing electricity costs for the average American to increase sharply. Some states have reported an increase of up to 36%, with wholesale power prices increasing by 267% in just five years. This is putting undue stress on the ordinary consumer, so much so that both sides of the aisle are demanding action from tech giants.Microsoft was the first to respond to Trump’s call for AI data centers to “pay their own way” when it comes to their electricity consumption, promising to be a “good neighbor” in the communities where it is present. OpenAI soon followed suit, saying that it will fund grid upgrades and apply flexible loads to reduce stress on the grid, while Anthropic said that it will pay 100% of its grid infrastructure costs and even produce new power sources. Now, it seems that the administration wants to formalize these promises. You may like Trump orders Big Tech to build its own power plants to generate power for AI data centers Trump says that AI tech companies need to ‘pay their own way’ when it comes to their electricity consumption Anthropic promises to pay for electricity price increases due to it's AI data centers Energy Secretary Chris Wright reportedly said in a statement that the pledge "will deliver more affordable, reliable, and secure energy for the American people and help stop the rising electricity prices that started during the previous administration," while ensuring the U.S. wins the AI race.However, experts are skeptical whether these promises and pledges will hold water in the long run. “The ratepayer protection plan is a show designed to sweep this issue under the rug and show the White House has solved the problem,” Director Ari Peskoe of the Harvard Law School Electricity Law Initiative told the publication. “The White House has no real authority here aside from the bully pulpit.” It’s not until we see big tech companies sign separate rate structures with electricity providers, grid operators, and state governments that we will know whether these pledges are effective. history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: 特朗普召集科技巨头赴白宫作出电力支付承诺——“纳税人保护计划”将要求数据中心运营商单独协议电力付费方案
history.descriptionZh:
包括亚马逊、Meta、微软和谷歌在内的全球主要人工智能科技公司，预计将于今日齐聚白宫，在美国前总统特朗普的主持下签署一项新的“费率缴纳者保护承诺”，旨在控制因AI数据中心耗电激增导致的电价上涨问题。这一行动源于特朗普在国情咨文中明确提出的要求：“我们正告知主要科技公司，他们有义务为自己的电力需求负责。”据彭博社报道，Meta、亚马逊和OpenAI已确认出席周三的白宫活动，而谷歌则拒绝对此置评。

AI数据中心的巨大电力需求已经颠覆了电力市场，导致美国普通民众的电费急剧上升。部分州报告的电价涨幅高达36%，而批发电力价格在短短五年内飙升了267%。这给普通消费者带来了过度压力，以至于美国两党都要求科技巨头采取行动。

微软率先响应了特朗普关于AI数据中心应为其电力消耗“自负其责”的呼吁，承诺在其运营所在的社区成为一个“好邻居”。OpenAI随即跟进，表示将资助电网升级并应用柔性负载以减轻电网压力。Anthropic则承诺将承担100%的电网基础设施成本，甚至开发新的电力来源。目前，政府方面似乎希望将这些承诺正式化。

据报道，能源部长克里斯·赖特在一份声明中表示，该承诺“将为美国人民提供更可负担、可靠和安全的能源，并有助于遏制始于上一届政府任内的电价上涨趋势”，同时确保美国在AI竞赛中获胜。

然而，专家们对这些承诺的长期有效性持怀疑态度。哈佛法学院电力法倡议主任阿里·佩斯科告诉媒体：“费率缴纳者保护计划是一场作秀，旨在掩盖问题，并表明白宫已解决此事。除了利用其舆论影响力，白宫在此事上并无实权。”只有当看到大型科技公司与电力供应商、电网运营商以及州政府签署独立的费率结构协议时，我们才能判断这些承诺是否真正有效。

（注：本文内容基于Tom's Hardware的报道生成，反映了相关政治声明、企业承诺及专家观点。事件背景涉及美国国内政策与行业动态。）
